text:
Now I have the complete picture. Here's the analysis:

---

## Public functions in `runtime` with no external callers

### Truly module-internal (never called outside their defining file)

| # | Function | File:Line | Verdict |
|---|----------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | `validate_read_only` | `bash_validation.rs:103` | **Internal helper** — only called by `validate_mode` in the same file. Could be `pub(crate)` or private. |
| 2 | `check_destructive` | `bash_validation.rs:241` | **Internal helper** — only called by `validate_command`. |
| 3 | `validate_mode` | `bash_validation.rs:284` | **Internal helper** — only called by `validate_command`. |
| 4 | `validate_sed` | `bash_validation.rs:336` | **Internal helper** — only called by `validate_command`. |
| 5 | `validate_paths` | `bash_validation.rs:360` | **Internal helper** — only called by `validate_command`. |
| 6 | `classify_command` | `bash_validation.rs:533` | **Dead code** — no callers at all besides in-file tests. |
| 7 | `validate_command` | `bash_validation.rs:594` | **Dead code** — the top-level entry point has no callers outside in-file tests. Not re-exported in `lib.rs`, not called from any other module. |
| 8 | `read_file_in_workspace` | `file_ops.rs:560` | **Dead code** — zero callers outside in-file tests. The non-workspace `read_file` is used instead. |
| 9 | `write_file_in_workspace` | `file_ops.rs:575` | **Dead code** — literally zero callers anywhere (not even tests). |
| 10 | `edit_file_in_workspace` | `file_ops.rs:589` | **Dead code** — literally zero callers anywhere (not even tests). |
| 11 | `is_symlink_escape` | `file_ops.rs:605` | **Dead code** — only in-file tests. Likely intended for future sandbox hardening. |
| 12 | `compress_summary` | `summary_compression.rs:37` | **Internal helper** — called only by `compress_summary_text` in the same file. The `_text` variant is the one used externally. |
| 13 | `should_enable` | `remote.rs:123` | **Internal method** — called only by `state_for_port` in the same struct. |
| 14 | `state_for_port` | `remote.rs:136` | **Internal method** — called only in in-file tests. |
| 15 | `subprocess_env` | `remote.rs:161` | **Internal method** — called only in in-file tests. |
| 16 | `fork_managed_session` | `session_control.rs:275` | **Convenience wrapper** — delegates to `fork_managed_session_for`. No external callers use this variant. |
| 17 | `is_session_reference_alias` | `session_control.rs:305` | **Internal helper** — called only by `resolve_session_reference_for` in the same file. |
| 18 | `managed_sessions_dir_for` | `session_control.rs:83` | **Internal helper** — called only by other functions in `session_control.rs`. |
| 19 | `load_managed_session` | `session_control.rs:256` | **Convenience wrapper** — delegates to `load_managed_session_for`. No external callers. |

### Re-exported in `lib.rs` but no external call sites found

These are part of the crate's public API surface but currently uncalled from any other crate:

| # | Function | File:Line | Verdict |
|---|----------|-----------|---------|
| 20 | `upstream_proxy_ws_url` | `remote.rs:201` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called by `ws_url()` method internally. Likely kept public for CLI extensibility. |
| 21 | `no_proxy_list` | `remote.rs:214` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called internally by `state_for_port` and `disabled`. |
| 22 | `inherited_upstream_proxy_env` | `remote.rs:221` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called in in-file tests. |
| 23 | `read_token` | `remote.rs:185` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called internally by `from_env_map`. |
| 24 | `code_challenge_s256` | `oauth.rs:255` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called by `generate_pkce_pair` internally. Useful as a standalone utility. |
| 25 | `parse_oauth_callback_query` | `oauth.rs:311` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called by `parse_oauth_callback_request_target` internally. |
| 26 | `credentials_path` | `oauth.rs:265` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called by `load/save/clear_oauth_credentials` internally. |
| 27 | `detect_container_environment` | `sandbox.rs:109` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called by `resolve_sandbox_status` internally. The `_from` variant is the testable one. |
| 28 | `prepend_bullets` | `prompt.rs:195` | **API surface, unused** — re-exported, only called within `prompt.rs` itself. |

### Summary

- **Items 1-19**: Truly dead or internal-only. These could safely be made `pub(crate)` or private. Items 9 and 10 (`write_file_in_workspace`, `edit_file_in_workspace`) have zero callers anywhere — they're the most clearly dead code.
- **Items 20-28**: Re-exported in `lib.rs` as public API but with no current external consumers. These are "unused-but-intentional" — they decompose internal logic into independently useful building blocks. Whether to keep them public depends on whether you want a stable utility API or a minimal surface area.
